task_completion.description: Navigate to the Edge flags configuration page
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Microsoft Edge browser window being open with 'edge://flags' entered in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Configure Smooth Scrolling and Parallel downloading flags
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Edge flags page where the dropdown menu for 'Smooth Scrolling' is set to 'Disabled', and/or 'Parallel downloading' is set to 'Enabled'.

task_completion.description: Configure Auto Dark Mode and QUIC protocol flags
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Edge flags page where the dropdown menu for 'Auto Dark Mode for Web Contents' is set to 'Enabled', and/or 'Experimental QUIC protocol' is set to 'Enabled'.

task_completion.description: Configure Tab Hover Card Images flag
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Edge flags page where the dropdown menu for 'Tab Hover Card Images' is set to 'Disabled'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the flag changes take effect by restarting the browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the blue 'Restart' button at the bottom right of the Edge window being clicked, or the browser closing and reopening immediately after the flags were modified.

task_completion.description: Create the configuration document with the correct flags and states
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the creation and contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/edge_flags_config.txt'. The file must list all 5 flags (Smooth Scrolling, Parallel downloading, Auto Dark Mode, QUIC protocol, Tab Hover Card Images) and correctly state their updated statuses (Disabled, Enabled, Enabled, Enabled, Disabled).

integrity.description: The agent used the Edge browser GUI to modify the flags
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent actually opens Microsoft Edge and uses the graphical interface to change the flags. It should not use terminal commands or scripts to directly edit the '~/.config/microsoft-edge/Local State' JSON file.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the configuration document
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent creates the text file manually using a text editor (like gedit or nano) or standard terminal commands (like echo), rather than running a pre-made script that bypasses the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring five specific flags in Microsoft Edge via the 'edge://flags' page: 1. Smooth Scrolling -> Disabled. 2. Parallel downloading -> Enabled. 3. Auto Dark Mode for Web Contents -> Enabled. 4. Experimental QUIC protocol -> Enabled. 5. Tab Hover Card Images -> Disabled. The changes must be applied, which in Edge requires clicking the 'Restart' button that appears at the bottom of the page after modifying a flag. Finally, a text file must be created exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/edge_flags_config.txt' listing these 5 flags and their new states.
